title: Olympiastadion Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **The stadium built for the 1936 Nazi Olympics is still standing and still in weekly use.** It survived the war almost intact, and today Hertha BSC play here, the German cup final lands every May, and big concerts fill the summer. You can walk right in on normal days.

**A self-guided walk-in costs 11 EUR (8 EUR reduced, 6 EUR for children), bought at the visitor centre at Olympischer Platz 3.** Open days run roughly 11:00 to 18:00, but hours change at short notice, so check olympiastadion.berlin before you travel out.

**The one thing that catches people out: on Hertha home games and major events the visitor centre closes and walk-ins stop.** On those days only guided tours run, and they sell out, so book ahead or plan to see the match instead.

**Inside you get the bowl, the running track, and the Marathon Gate with its eternal-flame cauldron and the cracked 1936 Olympic bell on the ground.** This is where Jesse Owens won four golds in front of the Nazi leadership. A 4 EUR multimedia guide walks you round in about 75 to 100 minutes.

**Getting there is easy and cheap: S-Bahn S3 or S9 to S Olympiastadion, or U2 to Olympia-Stadion.** Both stations sit inside Berlin AB, so a normal city ticket covers the trip. For the widest view, the separate Glockenturm bell tower looks out over the whole Olympic Park.
